inputs.userGoal: Create a real-time document Q&A API leveraging Azure OpenAI and Azure Cosmos DB, expose endpoints using Azure API Management, and enforce rate limits per user.
subGoals: Enterprise-grade security with private networking and managed identity
Per-user rate limiting via APIM policies
Vector search in Cosmos DB for document retrieval

resources.name: vnet-docqa-prod-eastus2

reasoning.whyChosen: Foundation for network isolation. All backend services connect via private endpoints in snet-pe. Container Apps environment uses delegated snet-aca (/23 for workload profiles). APIM uses snet-apim for outbound VNet integration.
alternativesConsidered: Hub-spoke topology with Azure Firewall â€” overkill for single-workload deployment
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single VNet simplifies DNS and routing but limits blast-radius isolation between workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: NSGs must exist before subnet association.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for Container Apps subnet to control inbound/outbound traffic. Allows VNet-internal traffic and Azure Load Balancer probes.
alternativesConsidered: No NSG â€” violates WAF security pillar
reasoning.tradeoffs: Permissive VNet rule; tighten for stricter environments.

reasoning.whyChosen: Restricts private endpoint subnet to HTTPS-only from within VNet. Explicit deny-all catches any unmatched traffic.
alternativesConsidered: Open NSG â€” violates least-privilege principle
reasoning.tradeoffs: Only port 443 allowed; Cosmos DB also uses 443 so this is sufficient.

reasoning.whyChosen: APIM management plane requires port 3443 from ApiManagement service tag. Client traffic on 443.
alternativesConsidered: No NSG â€” APIM requires explicit rules for management operations
reasoning.tradeoffs: Internet inbound on 443 is needed for public-facing gateway; restrict with IP ranges if client base is known.

reasoning.whyChosen: Central observability sink for all diagnostic settings, Container Apps system logs, and Application Insights backend. PerGB2018 is the recommended default SKU. 90-day retention balances cost with operational needs.
alternativesConsidered: CapacityReservation â€” better for >100 GB/day; not expected at this scale
reasoning.tradeoffs: Daily quota of 5 GB caps unexpected log spikes but may drop data during incidents; adjust based on observed volume.

resources.name: appi-docqa-prod-eastus2

reasoning.whyChosen: Workspace-based Application Insights provides distributed tracing, live metrics, and dependency tracking for the Container App and APIM. Classic mode is retired; workspace-based is mandatory for new resources.
alternativesConsidered: Standalone classic App Insights â€” retired, not an option
reasoning.tradeoffs: Local auth disabled for security; services must use managed identity or connection string from Key Vault.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Workspace-based App Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for Azure OpenAI private endpoint DNS resolution. Allows VNet-connected resources to resolve the OpenAI endpoint to the private IP.
alternativesConsidered: Custom DNS server â€” adds complexity without benefit in single-VNet topology
reasoning.tradeoffs: Global resource; one zone can serve multiple VNets via VNet links.

references.title: Private DNS zone for Cognitive Services

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for Cosmos DB NoSQL private endpoint DNS resolution.
alternativesConsidered: Service endpoints â€” deprecated path for Cosmos DB; private endpoints are WAF-recommended
reasoning.tradeoffs: None significant.

references.title: Private DNS zone for Cosmos DB

reasoning.whyChosen: Required for Key Vault private endpoint DNS resolution.
reasoning.tradeoffs: None.

references.title: Private DNS zones for Key Vault

resources.name: pdnsz-link-openai

reasoning.whyChosen: Links the OpenAI private DNS zone to the VNet so private endpoint DNS records are resolvable from within the network.
reasoning.tradeoffs: None.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both the DNS zone and VNet to exist.

resources.name: pdnsz-link-cosmos

reasoning.whyChosen: Links the Cosmos DB private DNS zone to the VNet.
reasoning.tradeoffs: None.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both the DNS zone and VNet to exist.

resources.name: pdnsz-link-keyvault

reasoning.whyChosen: Links the Key Vault private DNS zone to the VNet.
reasoning.tradeoffs: None.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both the DNS zone and VNet to exist.

resources.name: kv-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized secret management for API keys, connection strings, and certificates. RBAC authorization is WAF-recommended over access policies. Soft delete + purge protection guard against accidental/malicious deletion. Public access disabled; all access via private endpoint.
alternativesConsidered: Premium SKU â€” only needed for HSM-backed keys; not required here
Azure App Configuration â€” complementary for feature flags but doesn't replace Key Vault for secrets
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard SKU lacks HSM support; acceptable for API keys and connection strings.

resources.dependencyReasoning: VNet must exist for private endpoint connectivity.

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-eastus2

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure OpenAI provides enterprise-grade GPT models for document Q&A. S0 is the only SKU for OpenAI. Local auth disabled forces managed identity usage. Public access disabled; traffic flows through private endpoint only.
alternativesConsidered: Azure AI Services multi-service account â€” unnecessary; single-purpose OpenAI account is cleaner
Third-party LLM APIs â€” no enterprise SLA or data residency guarantees
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single-region deployment; add a second region behind APIM load balancing for higher availability if needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: VNet must exist for private endpoint connectivity.

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-eastus2/text-embedding-3-small

reasoning.whyChosen: text-embedding-3-small provides high-quality 1536-dimension embeddings at low cost and high throughput. Used to vectorize documents and queries for Cosmos DB vector search. GlobalStandard deployment type for pay-per-token flexibility.
alternativesConsidered: text-embedding-3-large â€” higher dimensions (3072) but 2x cost; small is sufficient for most Q&A
text-embedding-ada-002 â€” legacy; text-embedding-3-small outperforms at lower cost
reasoning.tradeoffs: 120K TPM capacity; increase if ingestion volume is high.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Model deployment requires the parent OpenAI account.

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-eastus2/gpt-4o

reasoning.whyChosen: GPT-4o offers the best quality/cost/speed balance for document Q&A. Handles complex reasoning over retrieved context with 128K token window. GlobalStandard for pay-per-token flexibility and global routing.
alternativesConsidered: gpt-4o-mini â€” 60% cheaper but weaker at multi-document synthesis
gpt-4 â€” legacy; gpt-4o is faster and cheaper at same quality
ProvisionedManaged â€” better for sustained high throughput; switch if >50K TPM steady-state
reasoning.tradeoffs: 80K TPM capacity; monitor usage and scale up or switch to PTU if latency variance is unacceptable.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Model deployment requires the parent OpenAI account.

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod
resources.subtype: Cosmos DB NoSQL with Vector Search

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL with vector search capability stores documents, metadata, and embeddings in a single database. Enables low-latency vector similarity search for RAG pattern. Session consistency balances performance and correctness for user-scoped Q&A. Zone-redundant for reliability. Continuous backup enables point-in-time restore.
alternativesConsidered: Azure AI Search â€” powerful but adds another service and cost; Cosmos DB vector search consolidates storage + search
PostgreSQL with pgvector â€” viable but lacks Cosmos DB's global distribution and autoscale
Azure SQL â€” no native vector search support
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single-region; add a second region if global availability is required. Vector search indexes are immutable after creation â€” plan partition key and index strategy carefully.

resources.dependencyReasoning: VNet must exist for private endpoint connectivity.

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod/docqa

reasoning.whyChosen: Logical database container for all Q&A collections.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single database; add more if multi-tenant isolation is needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of the Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod/docqa/documents

reasoning.whyChosen: Stores documents with 1536-dim vector embeddings for RAG. DiskANN index for high-performance ANN search. Autoscale 400-4000 RU/s. Embedding path excluded from standard index to reduce RU cost.
alternativesConsidered: quantizedFlat â€” better for smaller datasets
Separate embeddings container â€” adds complexity
reasoning.tradeoffs: DiskANN index is immutable. Autoscale max 4000 RU/s is conservative; increase based on load.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of the SQL database.

resources.name: cae-docqa-prod-eastus2

reasoning.whyChosen: Internal Container Apps environment keeps the API backend private â€” only accessible from within the VNet (via APIM). Zone-redundant for reliability. Dedicated D4 workload profile provides consistent CPU/memory for latency-sensitive Q&A requests. Consumption profile available for background/batch tasks.
alternativesConsidered: Azure App Service â€” lacks VNet-native isolation and KEDA scaling
Azure Kubernetes Service â€” full Kubernetes overhead not justified for single API
Azure Functions â€” cold starts problematic for real-time Q&A
reasoning.tradeoffs: Dedicated workload profile has baseline cost even at zero traffic; Consumption-only is cheaper but has cold starts.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires VNet subnet for network integration and Log Analytics workspace for logging.

resources.name: ca-docqa-api-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Hosts the document Q&A API backend. Internal ingress ensures only APIM can reach it. System-assigned managed identity authenticates to OpenAI, Cosmos DB, and Key Vault without credentials. Dedicated D4 profile avoids cold starts. Min 2 replicas for availability; HTTP scaling handles load spikes.
alternativesConsidered: Single replica â€” no availability during updates/failures
External ingress â€” exposes API directly, bypassing APIM rate limiting
reasoning.tradeoffs: 2 CPU / 4 GiB per container; right-size based on load testing. Image placeholder must be replaced with actual ACR image.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Container Apps environment for hosting, and references OpenAI/Cosmos DB endpoints and App Insights connection string.

resources.name: apim-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: APIM is the single entry point for the Q&A API. Standard v2 supports outbound VNet integration to reach the internal Container App, subscription-key and OAuth-based rate limiting, Application Insights integration, and caching. Per-user rate limiting enforced via rate-limit-by-key policy keyed on JWT claim or subscription key.
alternativesConsidered: Premium v2 â€” full VNet injection + zones; more expensive; use if multi-region or strict network isolation is required
Azure Front Door + App Gateway â€” adds latency and complexity for single-region
Consumption tier â€” no VNet integration, limited policies
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard v2 uses outbound VNet integration via delegated subnet. Single-region, no AZ support. Upgrade to Premium v2 for zone redundancy. Publisher email is placeholder.

resources.dependencyReasoning: VNet required for outbound integration to reach internal Container App. App Insights for API analytics and diagnostics.

resources.name: apim-docqa-prod/docqa-backend

properties.description: Internal Container App hosting the Document Q&A API

reasoning.whyChosen: APIM backend resource pointing to internal Container App FQDN. Routes all requests via VNet-integrated subnet.
alternativesConsidered: Direct URL in policy â€” backend resource is more maintainable
reasoning.tradeoffs: Backend URL depends on Container Apps environment default domain; resolved after deployment.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires APIM service and Container App.

resources.name: apim-docqa-prod/docqa-api

properties.description: Real-time document Q&A API powered by Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB

inbound.name: validate-jwt
inbound.description: Validates Entra ID JWT tokens for user authentication

inbound.name: rate-limit-by-key
inbound.description: Per-user rate limit: 30 requests/minute keyed on JWT subject claim

inbound.name: set-backend-service
inbound.description: Routes to internal Container App backend

reasoning.whyChosen: Defines the public API surface. validate-jwt authenticates Entra ID users. rate-limit-by-key enforces 30 req/min per user via JWT sub claim. quota-by-key caps daily usage at 1000 requests. set-backend-service routes to internal Container App.
alternativesConsidered: Subscription-key-only rate limiting â€” less granular per-user control
IP-based limiting â€” unreliable for mobile/NAT users
reasoning.tradeoffs: JWT validation adds ~1-2ms latency. Tenant ID and API app ID are placeholders. Rate limits are defaults; adjust per business requirements.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires APIM service and backend resource.

resources.name: pe-oai-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Private endpoint for Azure OpenAI ensures all API traffic stays within the VNet. DNS zone group auto-registers the A record in the private DNS zone.
alternativesConsidered: Service endpoints â€” not supported for Cognitive Services
Public access with IP restrictions â€” less secure than private endpoint
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumes one IP from snet-pe per endpoint.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the OpenAI account, VNet subnet, and linked DNS zone.

resources.name: pe-cosmos-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Private endpoint for Cosmos DB NoSQL API. GroupId 'Sql' is correct for the NoSQL (formerly SQL) API.
alternativesConsidered: VNet service endpoint â€” private endpoint is WAF-preferred
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cosmos DB may create multiple regional endpoints; additional PEs needed for multi-region.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the Cosmos DB account, VNet subnet, and linked DNS zone.

resources.name: pe-kv-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Private endpoint for Key Vault ensures secret retrieval stays within the VNet.
alternativesConsidered: VNet service endpoint for Key Vault â€” private endpoint is WAF-preferred
reasoning.tradeoffs: None significant.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Key Vault, VNet subnet, and linked DNS zone.

resources.name: ra-ca-to-openai

reasoning.whyChosen: Grants the Container App's managed identity permission to call OpenAI inference endpoints (completions, embeddings). 'Cognitive Services OpenAI User' is the least-privilege role for inference-only access.
alternativesConsidered: Cognitive Services OpenAI Contributor â€” grants model management; not needed for inference
API key auth â€” less secure; local auth is disabled on the account
reasoning.tradeoffs: None; this is the WAF-recommended pattern. In Bicep/ARM, resource name must be a deterministic GUID, e.g. guid(scope, principalId, roleDefinitionId).

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both the Container App (for principal ID) and OpenAI account (for scope).

resources.name: ra-ca-to-keyvault
resources.subtype: Role Assignment (Container App â†’ Key Vault)

reasoning.whyChosen: Grants the Container App's managed identity read-only access to Key Vault secrets. 'Key Vault Secrets User' is the least-privilege role for secret retrieval.
alternativesConsidered: Key Vault Administrator â€” too broad; app only needs to read secrets
Access policies â€” RBAC is WAF-recommended
reasoning.tradeoffs: None; standard pattern. In Bicep/ARM, resource name must be a deterministic GUID, e.g. guid(scope, principalId, roleDefinitionId).

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both the Container App (for principal ID) and Key Vault (for scope).

resources.name: ra-apim-to-keyvault

reasoning.whyChosen: Allows APIM to retrieve named values and certificates from Key Vault via managed identity.
alternativesConsidered: Direct secret embedding in APIM â€” insecure and hard to rotate
reasoning.tradeoffs: None. In Bicep/ARM, resource name must be a deterministic GUID, e.g. guid(scope, principalId, roleDefinitionId).

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires both APIM (for principal ID) and Key Vault (for scope).

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod/data-contributor-ca

reasoning.whyChosen: Native Cosmos SQL role assignment using built-in Data Contributor. Correct resource type for data-plane RBAC (not Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments).
alternativesConsidered: Azure RBAC â€” only works for control plane, not data plane with disableLocalAuth
reasoning.tradeoffs: Scoped to entire account; narrow to database/container for stricter least-privilege.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Container App managed identity and Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: ra-ca-acrpull

reasoning.whyChosen: Grants Container App managed identity permission to pull images from private ACR. AcrPull is least-privilege.
alternativesConsidered: Admin credentials â€” insecure
reasoning.tradeoffs: ACR must exist before this assignment. ACR provisioning is out of scope. In Bicep/ARM, resource name must be a deterministic GUID.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Container App for principal ID. ACR is an external dependency.

resources.name: diag-oai-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Captures OpenAI request/response logs, audit events, and metrics for monitoring token usage, latency, and content filter triggers.
reasoning.tradeoffs: RequestResponse logs may have volume cost; disable if cost is a concern and App Insights telemetry is sufficient.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the OpenAI account and Log Analytics workspace.

resources.name: diag-cosmos-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Captures Cosmos DB data plane requests, query performance, partition key statistics, and RU consumption for performance tuning and alerting on 429 throttling.
reasoning.tradeoffs: DataPlaneRequests log can be high volume; consider sampling or disabling for cost optimization.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Cosmos DB account and Log Analytics workspace.

resources.name: diag-apim-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Captures APIM gateway logs for request tracing, rate limit violations, and backend errors. Complements App Insights integration for end-to-end observability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: GatewayLogs include full request/response headers; exclude body logging for PII compliance.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires APIM service and Log Analytics workspace.

resources.name: diag-kv-docqa-prod

reasoning.whyChosen: Audit logging for Key Vault tracks all access and modification events â€” critical for security monitoring and compliance.
reasoning.tradeoffs: None; audit logging should always be enabled.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires Key Vault and Log Analytics workspace.

assumptions: Single-region deployment in East US 2; multi-region can be added later by replicating OpenAI + Cosmos DB and adding APIM multi-region or Front Door.
Container image will be built and pushed to an Azure Container Registry (ACR) â€” ACR provisioning is out of scope for this plan but should be added if not pre-existing.
Per-user rate limiting is enforced at APIM layer using rate-limit-by-key policy keyed on JWT sub claim or APIM subscription key.
Vector embeddings use 1536 dimensions (text-embedding-3-small default) with DiskANN index in Cosmos DB for efficient similarity search.
The Q&A API implements a RAG pattern: embed query â†’ vector search Cosmos DB â†’ augment prompt with context â†’ call GPT-4o for answer.
Publisher email in APIM is a placeholder and must be replaced.
Global unique resource names (Cosmos DB, Key Vault, APIM, OpenAI custom subdomain) use the naming pattern shown but may need suffixes to avoid collisions.
ACR is a pre-existing external dependency; not provisioned by this plan.
Entra ID tenant ID and API app ID for JWT validation are placeholders.
Role assignment names use friendly identifiers for readability; actual Bicep/ARM must use deterministic GUIDs.
Per-user rate limiting: 30 req/min and 1000 req/day as defaults; adjust per business requirements.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture implements a secure, enterprise-grade document Q&A API using the RAG pattern. Azure OpenAI provides embedding and completion models. Cosmos DB NoSQL with vector search stores documents and embeddings in a single store, eliminating the need for a separate search service. Container Apps hosts the API backend in an internal VNet-integrated environment, ensuring it's only reachable via APIM. API Management provides the public-facing gateway with per-user rate limiting (rate-limit-by-key policy), subscription management, and caching. All backend services are connected via private endpoints with dedicated DNS zones. Managed identity with least-privilege RBAC eliminates credential management. Application Insights and Log Analytics provide end-to-end observability.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: 1) Single-region: no geo-redundancy for OpenAI or APIM; acceptable for initial deployment. 2) Standard v2 APIM lacks availability zones; upgrade to Premium v2 for zone redundancy. 3) Cosmos DB vector search is newer than Azure AI Search; AI Search has richer features (semantic ranking, skills) but adds cost and complexity. 4) Container Apps dedicated profile has baseline cost; switch to Consumption-only if cold starts are acceptable. 5) Role assignment names must be GUIDs in IaC. 6) ACR assumed pre-existing.

plan.validation: All resources have valid ARM types, CAF-aligned names, and complete dependency chains. Cosmos DB database and container with vector search policy (DiskANN, 1536-dim, cosine) are explicitly modeled. APIM includes backend, API with 6 operations, JWT validation, and per-user rate-limit-by-key + quota-by-key policies. Container App has proper ACR registry auth via managed identity and Key Vault-backed secret references. Cosmos data-plane RBAC uses native sqlRoleAssignments. Deployment order: NSGs -> VNet -> Log Analytics -> App Insights -> DNS Zones -> DNS Links -> Key Vault -> OpenAI Account -> OpenAI Deployments -> Cosmos DB Account -> Database -> Container -> Container Apps Env -> Container App -> APIM -> Backend -> API -> Private Endpoints -> Role Assignments -> Diagnostic Settings. No circular dependencies.
architecturePrinciples: Zero-trust networking: all backend services use private endpoints; no public access
Managed identity everywhere: no stored credentials; RBAC for all service-to-service auth
Defense in depth: NSGs + private endpoints + disabled public access + Key Vault for any remaining secrets
Observability by default: diagnostic settings on every resource, centralized Log Analytics + App Insights
Least privilege: each role assignment grants minimum required permissions
Cost awareness: pay-per-token OpenAI, autoscale Cosmos DB, HTTP-based Container Apps scaling
